skill.name: langfuse
skill.description: Expert in Langfuse - the open-source LLM observability platform. Covers tracing, prompt management, evaluation, datasets, and integration with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and OpenAI.
skill.summary: LLM observability and tracing expertise for debugging, monitoring, and improving AI application performance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 17 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for Langfuse, an open-source LLM observability platform. External command detections are Python code examples, not shell execution. Network URLs are legitimate API endpoints. API key references are placeholder examples in documentation.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: Pattern matcher incorrectly identified Python code examples as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All flagged lines contain legitimate Python SDK usage for Langfuse tracing.
URLs point to cloud.langfuse.com, the official Langfuse cloud service endpoint. This is documented configuration for connecting to the observability platform, not suspicious network activity.
References to secret_key use placeholder values (sk-...) in documentation examples. These illustrate where users configure their API keys, not actual credential access.

content.user_title: Add LLM Observability and Tracing to Your AI Applications
content.value_statement: Debug and monitor your LLM applications with comprehensive tracing and evaluation. Langfuse provides open-source observability to track costs, latency, and quality across your AI workflows.
actual_capabilities: Instrument LLM calls with Langfuse tracing for OpenAI, LangChain, and LlamaIndex
Configure prompt management and versioning for iterative improvements
Set up evaluation pipelines to score and compare model outputs
Track token usage, costs, and latency across all LLM interactions
Create datasets for systematic testing and A/B testing of prompts
Debug production issues by analyzing traces, spans, and user sessions
limitations: Self-hosted deployment requires managing your own infrastructure
High-volume applications may need batch configuration tuning
Dashboard analytics may have slight latency for real-time monitoring
Evaluation scoring requires manual setup of criteria and thresholds

use_cases.title: Production LLM Application Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track and debug live AI applications with full trace visibility. Monitor token costs, response latency, and error rates across user sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers deploying LLM features to production

use_cases.title: Prompt Development and Testing
use_cases.description: Version prompts, compare outputs across iterations, and run A/B tests. Use datasets to systematically evaluate prompt changes before deployment.
use_cases.target_user: Prompt engineers iterating on AI behavior and responses

use_cases.title: AI Cost and Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Analyze token usage patterns, identify expensive operations, and optimize model selection. Set up alerts for cost anomalies and performance degradation.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering managers controlling AI infrastructure budgets

prompt_templates.title: Basic Tracing Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Langfuse tracing for my OpenAI application. I need to track chat completions with user IDs and session IDs. Show me the Python code to initialize Langfuse and wrap my existing OpenAI calls.

prompt_templates.title: LangChain Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a LangChain application with custom chains and agents. Guide me through adding Langfuse callback handlers to trace all chain executions, including nested calls and tool usage.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding observability to existing LangChain workflows

prompt_templates.title: Prompt Versioning and Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to manage multiple versions of my system prompts in Langfuse and compare their performance. Show me how to create prompts in Langfuse, retrieve them in code, and analyze which versions perform better.
prompt_templates.scenario: Iterative prompt development with A/B testing

prompt_templates.title: Custom Evaluation Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build an evaluation pipeline that scores my LLM outputs based on relevance and accuracy. I need to create test datasets, define scoring criteria, and run batch evaluations to compare model versions.

output_examples.input: User asks for help tracing an OpenAI chat application with Langfuse
output: Initialization code showing Langfuse client setup with API keys and host configuration
Trace creation with user_id, session_id, and metadata fields
Generation logging that captures input messages, model parameters, and token usage
Example of scoring traces based on user feedback or automated criteria

output_examples.input: User needs LangChain callback integration for their RAG pipeline
output: CallbackHandler setup with public key, secret key, and optional session tracking
Configuration showing how to pass handlers to chain.invoke() calls
Example of setting global callback handler for automatic tracing
Sample trace output showing nested spans for retriever and LLM calls

best_practices: Always call langfuse.flush() in serverless environments to ensure traces are sent before function exit
Include user_id and session_id on all traces to enable user-level debugging and session analytics
Use meaningful trace and span names that reflect business logic rather than generic function names
anti_patterns: Not flushing traces in serverless functions, causing data loss when execution ends
Tracing every operation without filtering, creating noise and performance overhead
Missing user and session identifiers, limiting ability to debug specific user experiences

faq.question: Is Langfuse free to use?
faq.answer: Langfuse offers a free tier for development and small-scale usage. Self-hosting is completely free and open-source. Cloud hosting has paid plans for higher volumes and additional features.

faq.question: Does Langfuse work with models other than OpenAI?
faq.answer: Yes. Langfuse supports OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, LlamaIndex, LangChain, and custom integrations. You can trace any LLM by using the manual tracing API with your own instrumentation.

faq.question: How do I handle sensitive data in traces?
faq.answer: Use the metadata masking feature to redact sensitive fields before sending to Langfuse. You can also self-host Langfuse to keep all trace data within your infrastructure.

faq.question: Can I use Langfuse with streaming responses?
faq.answer: Yes. Langfuse automatically handles streaming for supported integrations. For manual tracing, log the complete response after the stream finishes, or use incremental updates for long-running streams.

faq.question: How do I compare model performance across different providers?
faq.answer: Use Langfuse datasets to run the same test cases against different models. Tag traces with model names and use the dashboard to compare latency, cost, and quality scores side by side.

faq.question: What happens if Langfuse is unavailable during tracing?
faq.answer: The Langfuse SDK batches traces asynchronously and retries on failure. Your application continues normally even if Langfuse is temporarily unreachable. Traces are queued and sent when connectivity resumes.
